blocks.content: Cage B Team arrives on the mainland.
As the 21 magicians of Cage B Team walked down the road, people instinctively made way for them.
In a place with no rules except for the unspoken ones between communities, the inhabitants of the mainland had developed an almost evolutionary sense for detecting strength.
However, Cage B Team wasn't particularly trying to intimidate anyone.

Despite being a party composed of 4th to 6th rank magicians, they were mostly young, with only three members—Team Leader Rose, Deputy Leader Horkin, and Communication Magician Weygon—having previous experience in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: Ouch, ouch.
A petite woman with a bob haircut and bangs tied above her forehead kept scratching herself.
This was Aroella, a certified 4th rank fire magician known as the Flame of Fire.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: Why am I so itchy? Is it the air here?
Unable to bear it, she scratched herself directly under her clothes.

blocks.speaker: Summoning Magician Maloy
blocks.content: What's with you? Why do you keep groping yourself? Have you decided to be self-sufficient?

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: No… Ever since we got here, my armpits have been itchy. Beam, did some weird bug bite me?

blocks.speaker: Insect Magician Beam
blocks.content: It's because you don't shave.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: Very funny! I don't even have hair there!

blocks.speaker: Terminator Tarvan
blocks.content: That's even weirder.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: Shut up! I'm in a terrible mood, so don't mess with me! Or I'll burn you to a crisp!
Ignoring Aroella, Tarvan turned to Team Leader Rose.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: What now? Do we even know where to find Gaold? Should we just leave it to Jonah in the city center?

blocks.speaker: Deputy Leader Horkin
blocks.content: No. Even for a Cage-level team, the mainland isn't to be taken lightly. If we recklessly spread the Spirit Zone, we might attract unwanted attention and compromise the mission.

blocks.speaker: Carnivore addict Benifis
blocks.content: Old man, isn't it time for you to retire? We're the kingdom's elite force, capable of taking down demons. We don't need to be cautious.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: A Cage-level party can take down demons. But here, even demons form Cage-level groups.
A moment of silence followed.

blocks.speaker: Team Leader Rose
blocks.content: Heaven is a dangerous place. Especially since our target is Micaiah Gaold, the kingdom's top battle magician.

blocks.speaker: Word Magician Mami
blocks.content: So what? No matter how strong Gaold is, he can't take down a demon alone. We won't lose in combat strength.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Demons and humans are different. There are ways to strategize against demons, but humans have too many variables. And…

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Gaold can take them down alone.
Mami swallowed hard.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: The best outcome is to eliminate Gaold without any team casualties. But it's not unusual for someone to die. We're not talking about numbers for fun. Gaold is special.
The team's expressions finally showed their resolve.
In all their years together, it was the first time the team leader had placed the target above the team.
Meanwhile, Tarvan showed no signs of tension. As the team's most destructive magician, he couldn't afford to be intimidated before the fight even began.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: If we can't use the Spirit Zone, we'll have to do it the hard way.
Tarvan suddenly turned and headed into an alley.
Everyone knew they had been followed since arriving, so the team followed without complaint.
In the alley, armed men appeared.
There were as many of them as Cage B Team, and their eyes gleamed with murderous intent.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Who are you?
Mental Magician White connected a mental channel to translate Tarvan's words, but the armed robbers weren't surprised.

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: You don't know who we are? We pegged you as country bumpkins the moment you started looking around. Did you think you'd be safe in a group?

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: I'm asking because I don't know. Who are you?

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: Heh, let me give you some good advice. First, when someone calls you on the mainland, you run over and kneel. Second, you hand over everything you have. Third, leave the women and run for your lives. Now, get to it.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Here or there, it's all the same…
Five minutes later.
Twenty robbers lay on the alley floor, their faces smashed.
They were so weak that Tarvan hadn't even needed to use his signature move, the Kaiser Blast.

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: Please, spare me…
Tarvan grabbed the leader by the collar and lifted him.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Let me ask you something.

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: Yes, yes! Anything you want to know, I'll find out even if I don't know it!
It was almost laughable.
At least they were quicker to switch sides than the robbers in their original world.
Communication Magician Weygon smiled brightly and used magic to project an image in front of the leader.
When Gaold's face appeared, the leader's face turned pale with fear.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Have you seen this man?

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: Uh, well…
From the leader's reaction, Cage B Team realized he had seen Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Mental Magician White
blocks.content: Got lucky, Tarvan.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Tch, I already calculated this.
Tarvan's words were likely false, but the result made sense.
The lowest tier of the mainland's food chain targeted newcomers.
If they couldn't gauge their opponent's strength, they would have followed Gaold too.

blocks.speaker: White
blocks.content: Hey, you. Spill everything you know.
White's words conveyed through the mental channel revealed the leader's quick thinking.

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: I-I don't know anything. But…
White didn't listen and turned back to the team.

blocks.speaker: White
blocks.content: It's over. Let's discuss.
As Tarvan raised his fist with a grin, the leader, realizing things had gone wrong, shouted in despair.

blocks.speaker: The leader
blocks.content: Wait! I saw him! I did!

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: I know.
The leader's face was smashed as he was thrown against the wall.
Tarvan, shaking out his fist, looked at Horkin.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Disappointing. For a place where demons roam in Cage-level groups, these guys are pathetic.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Huh?
Etella, examining the hunting list Frankwine had given her, looked up at the strange feeling.
Twenty-one people occupied the alley.
Kuan instinctively placed his hand on his sword's hilt. The aura alone indicated they were all masters of their respective fields.
The tension skyrocketed, and dozens of eyes quickly assessed each other.
Tarvan had to swallow his earlier words.
He had thought this was a place for people, but even non-human-like beings could be encountered in the mainland.
Horkin stroked his beard with an intrigued smile.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Hmm, what to do?
For those trying to leave, this was a dead-end alley.
If they weren't going to fight, they would have to back up and make way, but no one in Cage B Team would consider such a ridiculous move.
Realizing the other group had no intention of stepping aside, Kuan slowly walked forward.
A limp.
All twenty-one eyes simultaneously examined Kuan's leg.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Is he going to try alone? Is he crazy?

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Unless he planned to hug and kiss them, there was no reason to approach in a blocked alley.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Ridiculous. If you want to leave, you go back.
As Tarvan exuded hostility to block the approach, Kuan's aura amplified like a chemical reaction.

blocks.speaker: Someone in the team
blocks.content: Wow…
No one even thought to scold him, as Tarvan's lips twitched.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: What a killer aura.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: There was no such thing as a friendly killer aura, but the lack of hesitation meant he was fully prepared.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Yes, this is the kind of resolve needed.
Kuan limped forward with even steps, never blinking as he stared at Tarvan.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: One more step.
At that point, he would have to cut.
The moment came quickly, and Kuan's sword created afterimages as it slashed, and Tarvan's body turned into a flash of light, retreating.

blocks.speaker: Someone in the team
blocks.content: Phew!

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: A swordsman's physical reaction speed is faster than a magician's. If Horkin hadn't intervened precisely, the outcome might have been different.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: Did I dodge? Or was I cut?
His nose felt cold as if it were frozen.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: It could have been the wind pressure or an actual cut. But checking with his hand would hurt his pride.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Huh? Are you from the continent?
Etella, surprised, pointed at Horkin.
Hearing a familiar language, Cage B Team's hostility slightly eased.
Of course, that didn't mean they would step aside or turn back.
But all twenty-five people in the alley instinctively knew that resolving this situation peacefully was best.
Horkin, skilled in diplomacy, stepped forward.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Ah, I didn't realize you were from the same place. Nice to meet you. Are you on a mission too?

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Yes, we almost ended up fighting each other.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Fighting each other, huh?
Horkin's lips curled into a slight smile.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: They weren't so clueless as to not recognize a Cage-level team when they saw one.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Are they from the Red Line?
Weygon, seizing the opportunity for reconciliation, approached. His actions were calculated, aiming to ease the tension.

blocks.speaker: Weygon
blocks.content: We're looking for someone. Have you seen this person?
When Gaold's face appeared in the magical vision, Etella's eyes widened in surprise.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Oh? Isn't this Micaiah Gaold?

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: I know him as the head of the Tormia Magic Association. Why are you looking for him here on the mainland?

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Hmm, so that's it…
Horkin blinked, deep in thought.
To be precise, Gaold was the former head of the Tormia Magic Association.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Do they really not know? They don't seem to have been on the mainland for long. Well, it's only been less than a month.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: It's nothing. Just something we need for our mission.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Alright then.
Etella bowed slightly, signaling that they should be allowed to pass.
Some of the younger members of Cage B Team felt a bit of resistance, but Rose's cold voice restored order.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: Let them through.
Cage B Team stepped aside, allowing Armin's group to pass.
Most avoided eye contact, but Aroella glared at Siina until the last moment.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: That woman is definitely an ice magician. I'm sure of it.

blocks.speaker: Aroella
blocks.content: Aroella's intuition, though subjective, was often accurate.
Once Armin's group was out of sight, Rose immediately turned to White.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: Well?

blocks.speaker: White
blocks.content: What do you think? Of course, it failed. They blocked all thoughts, so I couldn't read anything. And it wasn't a situation where I could aggressively probe.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: What about that man?

blocks.speaker: White
blocks.content: He did follow Gaold. But Gaold didn't respond and left the mainland immediately. He had two companions: Kangnan and an unknown woman.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: An unknown woman? Hmm.

blocks.speaker: White
blocks.content: According to his memory, they didn't enter any buildings or rest. They left within an hour of arriving.

blocks.speaker: Rose
blocks.content: I see. Finding out why will be the quickest way to reach Gaold. We'll retrace their steps.
As Rose led the way out of the alley, the rest of the team followed.
Horkin suddenly stopped and looked back down the road.

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: Old man, what's wrong?

blocks.speaker: Horkin
blocks.content: The woman I shook hands with. I feel like I've seen her before, but I can't remember where. It wasn't in the magical society.

blocks.speaker: Tarvan
blocks.content: With skills like that, you've probably seen her somewhere. Anyway, let's go. I'm starving.
Horkin's gaze lingered on the path where Etella's group had disappeared for a long time.
